author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.

ccel.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

source.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

title: Chapter V. 19.

source.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: John the evangelist, among his fellows and companions the other evangelists, received this special and peculiar gift from the Lord (on whose breast he reclined at the feast, hereby to signify that he was drinking deeper secrets from His inmost heart), to utter those things concerning the Son of God which may perhaps rouse the attentive minds of the little ones, but cannot fill them, as yet not capable of receiving them; while to minds, of somewhat larger growth, and coming to a certain age of inner manhood, he gives in these words something whereby they may both be exercised and fed.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: You have heard it when it was read, and you remember how this discourse arose.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For yesterday it was read, that “therefore the Jews sought to kill Jesus, because He not only broke the Sabbath, but also said that God was His Father, making Himself equal with God.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This that displeased the Jews, pleased the Father.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This, without doubt, pleases them too that honor the Son as they honor the Father; for if it does not please them, they will not be pleasing.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For God will not be greater because it pleases thee, but thou wilt be less if it displeases thee.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Now against this calumny of theirs, coming either of ignorance or of malice, the Lord speaks not at all what they can understand, but that whereby they may be agitated and troubled, and, on being troubled, it may be, seek the Physician.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And He uttered what should be written, that it might afterwards be read even by us.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Now we have seen what happened in the hearts of the Jews when they heard these words; what happens in ourselves when we hear them, let us more fully consider.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For heresies, and certain tenets of perversity, ensnaring souls and hurling them into the deep, have not sprung up except when good Scriptures are not rightly understood, and when that in them which is not rightly understood is rashly and boldly asserted.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And so, dearly beloved, ought we very cautiously to hear those things for the understanding of which we are but little ones, and that, too, with pious heart and with trembling, as it is written, holding this rule of soundness, that we rejoice as in food in that which we have been able to understand, according to the faith with which we are imbued; and what we have not yet been able to understand, that we lay aside doubting, and defer the understanding of it for a time; that is, even if we do not yet know what it is, that still we doubt not in the least that it is good and true.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And as for me, brethren, you must consider who I am that undertake to speak to you, and what I have undertaken: for I have taken upon me to treat of things divine, being a man; of spiritual things, being carnal; of things eternal, being a mortal.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Also from me, dearly beloved, far be vain presumption, if my conversation would be sound in the house of God, “which is the Church of the living God, the pillar and foundation of the truth.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In proportion to my measure I take what I put before you: where it is opened, I see with you; where it is shut, I knock with you.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Now the Jews were moved and indignant: justly, indeed, because a man dared to make himself equal with God; but unjustly in this, because in the man they understood not the God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They saw the flesh, the God they knew not; they observed the habitation, of the inhabitant they were ignorant.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: That flesh was a temple, within it dwelt God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It was not the flesh that Jesus made equal to the Father, it was not the form of a servant that He compared to the Lord; not that which He became for us, but that which He was when He made us.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For who Christ is (I speak to Catholics) you know, because you have rightly believed; not Word only, nor flesh only, but the Word was made flesh to dwell among us.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I recite again concerning the Word what you know: “In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was God:” here is equality with the Father.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But “the Word was made flesh, and dwelt among us.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Than this flesh the Father is greater.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thus the Father is both equal and greater; equal to the Word, greater than the flesh; equal to Him by whom He made us, greater than He who was made for us.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: By this sound catholic rule, which you ought particularly to know, which you who know it hold fast, from which your faith ought not in any case to slip, which is to be wrested from your heart by no arguments of men, let us measure the things we do understand; and the things which, it may be, we do not understand, let us defer, to be hereafter measured by this rule, when we shall be competent to do this.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We know Him, then, as equal to the Father, the Son of God, because we know Him in the beginning as God the Word.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Why, then, sought the Jews to slay Him?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Because He not only broke the Sabbath, but also said that God was His Father, making Himself equal with God:” seeing the flesh, not seeing the Word.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let Him therefore speak against them, the Word through the flesh; let Him, the dweller within, speak for through His dwelling-place, that whoso can, shall know who He is that dwells within.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What saith He then to them?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Then answered Jesus, and said unto them,” being indignant because He made Himself equal with God, “Verily, verily, I say unto you, The Son cannot do anything of Himself, but what He seeth the Father doing.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What the Jews answered to these words is not written: and perhaps they said nothing.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Certain, however, who wish to be esteemed Christians, are not silent, but from these words somehow conceive certain opinions in contradiction to us, which are not to be despised, both for their and for our sakes.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Arian heretics, namely, while they assert that the Son, who took upon Himself flesh, is less than the Father, not by the flesh, but before taking flesh, and not of the same substance as the Father, take a handle of misrepresentation from these words, and reply to us: “You see that the Lord Jesus, observing the Jews to be moved with indignation at his making himself equal to God the Father, subjoined such words as these, to show that he was not equal with God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For the Jews,” say they, “were provoked against Christ, because he made him self equal with God; and Christ, wishing to cure them of this impression, and to show them that the Son is not equal to the Father, that is, to God, saith this, as if he said, Why are ye angry?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Why are ye indignant?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I am not equal to God, since ‘the Son cannot do anything of himself, except what he seeth the Father doing.’

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Now,” say they, “he who ‘cannot do anything of himself, but what he seeth the Father doing,’ is surely less, not equal.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In this distorted and depraved rule of his own heart, let the heretic hear us, not as yet chiding, but still as it were inquiring, and let him explain to us what he thinks.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For, I suppose, whoever thou art (for we may regard him as here present in person), thou dost hold with us, that “in the beginning was the Word.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I do hold it, saith he.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And that “the Word was with God”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This too, saith he, I hold.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Proceed then, and hold the stronger saying that follows, that “the Word was God.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Even this, says he, I hold: but yet, this, God the greater; that, God the less.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Now this somehow smells of the pagan: I thought I was speaking with a Christian.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If there is God the greater, and God the less, then we worship two Gods, not one God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Why, saith he; dost not thou, too, affirm two Gods, equal the one to the other?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This I do not assert: for I understand this equality as implying therein also undivided love; and if undivided love, then perfect unity.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For if the love that God put in men doth make of many hearts of men one heart, and doth make many souls of men into one soul, as it is written of them that believed and mutually loved one another, in the Acts of the Apostles, “They had one soul and one heart toward God:” if, therefore, my soul and thy soul become one soul, when we think the same thing and love one another, how much more must God the Father and God the Son be one God in the fountain of love!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But to these words, by which thy heart is disturbed, bend thy thought, and reflect with me on that which we were seeking out concerning the Word.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We already hold that “the Word was God:” I join to this another thing, that, having said, “This was in the beginning with God,” the evangelist immediately subjoined, “All things were made by Him.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Now will I urge thee by questioning, now will I move thee against thyself, and sue thee against thyself: only keep this in memory concerning the Word, that “the Word was God, and all things were made by Him.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hear now the words by which thou wast moved to assert that the Son is less, forsooth, because He said, “The Son cannot of Himself do anything, but what He seeth the Father doing.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Just so, saith he.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Explain to me this a little: This is, I presume, how thou thinkest: that the Father doeth certain things, and the Son observes how the Father doeth, that He may also Himself be able to do those things which He seeth the Father doing.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thou hast set up two artisans, as it were: the Father and the Son just like master and learner, like as artisan fathers are wont to teach their sons their craft.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Behold, I come down to thy carnal sense: for the moment I think as thou doest: let us see if this our conception finds an issue in harmony with the things which we have just now alike spoken and alike hold regarding the Word, that “the Word was God,” and that “all things were made by Him.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Suppose, then, the Father, as an artisan, doing certain works, and the Son as a learner, who “cannot of Himself do anything, but what He seeth the Father doing:” He keenly watches, in a manner, the Father’s hands, that, as He seeth Him fashioning aught, so He may Himself in like manner fashion something similar by His own works.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But the Father here doeth all those things that He doeth, and wishes the Son to give heed to Him, and to do the like also Himself; by whom doeth the Father?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Come! now is the time for thee to stand to thy former opinion, which thou didst recite with me, and didst hold with me; that “in the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was God, and all things were made by Him.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But thou, after holding with me, that all things were made by the Word, dost again, with thy carnal wit and childish fancy, imagine with thyself God making something, and the Word giving heed; so that when God has made, the Word also may make the like.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Now, what does God make without the Word?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For if He doeth aught, then were not all things made by the Word; thou hast given up the position which thou didst hold.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But if all things were made by the Word, correct what thou didst understand amiss.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Father made, and made only by the Word: in what way does the Word give heed to see the Father making without the Word, what the Word may do in like manner?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Whatever the Father hath made, He made it by the Word; else is it false that “all things were made by Him.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But it is true that “all things were made by Him.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Perhaps this did not seem enough for thee?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Well, “and without Him was nothing made.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Withdraw, then, from this wisdom of the flesh, and let us inquire in what manner it is said, “The Son cannot of Himself do anything, but what He seeth the Father doing.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let us inquire, if we are worthy to apprehend.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For I confess it is a great thing, and altogether difficult; to see the Father doing through the Son: not the Father and the Son doing each His particular works, but the Father doing every work whatsoever by the Son; so that not any works are done by the Father without the Son, or by the Son without the Father, because “all things were made by Him, and without Him was nothing made.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: These truths being most firmly established in the foundation of faith, what now is the nature of this “seeing”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thou seekest, as I suppose, to know the Son doing: seek first to know the Son seeing.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For what, in fact, saith He?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “The Son cannot of Himself do anything, but what He seeth the Father doing.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Note what He said, “but what He seeth the Father doing.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The seeing comes first, the doing follows: He seeth in order to do.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: As for thee, why seekest thou at present to know how He doeth, whilst thou understandest not as yet how He seeth?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Why runnest thou to that which comes later, leaving that which comes first?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He declares Himself as seeing and doing, not doing and seeing; because “He cannot of Himself do anything, but what He seeth the Father doing.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Wilt thou that I explain to thee how He doeth?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Do thou explain to me how He seeth.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If thou canst not explain this, neither can I that.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If thou art not yet competent to understand this, neither am I to understand that.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore let each of us seek, each knock, that each may merit to receive.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Why dost thou, as if thou wert learned, unjustly blame me who am unlearned?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I in respect of the doing, thou in respect of the seeing, being both unlearned, let us inquire of the Master, not childishly wrangle in His school.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We have already, however, learned together that “all things were made by Him.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore it is manifest that it is not a different kind of works that the Father doeth, that, seeing them, the Son may do other works like them; but the very same doeth the Father by the Son, because all things were made by the Word.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Now, as to how God doeth, who knows?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How made He, I will not say the world, but thine own eye, in thy carnal attachment to which thou comparest visible things with invisible?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For thou conceivest of God such things as thou art wont to see with these eyes.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But if God might be seen with these eyes, He would not have said, “Blessed are the pure in heart, for they shall see God.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Accordingly, thou hast an eye of the body to see an artificer, but thou hast not yet the eye of the heart to see God: hence, what thou art wont to see in an artificer, thou wouldest transfer to God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Leave earthly things on the earth; set thy heart on high.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What then, beloved, are we going to explain that which we have asked, how the Word seeth, how the Father is seen by the Word, what the seeing of the Word is?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I am not so bold, so rash, as to promise to explain this, for myself or for you: however I estimate your measure, still I know my own.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore, if you please, not to delay it longer, let us run over the passage, and see how carnal hearts are troubled by the words of the Lord; to this end troubled, that they may not continue in that which they hold.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let this be wrested from them, as some toy is wrested from children, with which they amuse themselves to their hurt, that, as persons of larger growth, they may have more profitable things planted in them, and may be able to make progress, instead of crawling on the earth.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Arise, seek, sigh, pant with desire, and knock at what is shut.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But if we do not yet desire, not yet earnestly seek, not yet sigh, we shall only be throwing pearls to all indiscriminately, or finding pearls ourselves, regardless of what kind.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore, beloved, I would move a longing desire in your heart.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Good character leads to right understanding: the kind of life leads to another kind of life.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: One kind of life is earthly, another is heavenly: there is a life of beasts, another of men, and another of angels.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The life of beasts is excited with earthly pleasures, seeks earthly pleasures alone, and grovels after them with immoderate desire: the life of angels is alone heavenly; the life of men is midway between that of angels and of beasts.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If man lives after the flesh, he is on a level with the beasts; if he lives after the Spirit, he joins in the fellowship of angels.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When thou livest after the Spirit, examine even in the angelic life whether thou be small or well-grown.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For if thou art still a little one, the angels say to thee, “Grow: we feed on bread; thou art nourished with milk, with the milk of faith that thou mayest come to the meat of sight.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But if there be still a longing for filthy pleasures, if the thoughts be still of deceit, if lies are not avoided, if perjuries be heaped on lies, shall a heart so foul dare to say, “Explain to me how the Word sees;” even if I be able to do so, even if I myself now see?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And further, though not perhaps of this character myself, and I am nevertheless far from this vision, how must that man be weighed down with earthly desires, who is not yet rapt with this desire from above!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: There is a wide difference between loathing and desiring; and again, between desiring and enjoying.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If thou livest as do the beasts, thou loathest: the angels have full enjoyment.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If, on the other hand, thou livest not as the beast, thou hast no longer loathing: something thou desirest, and dost not receive: thou hast, by the very desire, begun the life of the angels.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: May it grow in thee, and be perfected in thee; and mayest thou receive this, not of me, but of Him who made both me and thee!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Yet the Lord also has not left us to chance, since, in that He said, “The Son cannot of Himself do anything, but what He seeth the Father doing,” He meant us to understand that the Father doeth, not some works which the Son may see, and the Son doeth other works after He has seen the Father doing; but that both the Father and Son do the very same works.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For He goes on to say, “For what things soever He doeth, these also doeth the Son in like manner.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Not after the Father hath done works, doeth the Son other works in like manner; but, “whatever He doeth, these also the Son doeth in like manner.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If these the Son doeth which the Father doeth, then it is by the Son that the Father doeth: if by the Son the Father doeth what He doeth, then the Father doeth not some, the Son others; but the works of the Father and of the Son are the same works.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And how doeth the Son also the same?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Both “the same,” and “in like manner.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In case you should think them the same, but in a different manner, the “same,” saith He, and “in like manner.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And how could they be the same and not in like manner?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Take an example, which I presume is not too big for you: when we write letters they are first formed by our heart, then by our hand.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Certainly: why otherwise have you all agreed, but because you perceived it to be so?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It is as I have said, it is manifest to us all.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The letters are made first by our heart, then by our body; the hand serves, the heart commands; both the heart and the hand make the same letters.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Dost think the heart doeth some letters, the hand some others?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The same indeed doeth the hand, but not in like manner: our heart forms them intelligibly, but our hand visibly.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: See how the same things are made, but not in like manner.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hence it was not enough for the Lord to say, “What things soever the Father doeth, these also the Son doeth;” He must add, “and in like manner.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For what if thou shouldst understand this just as thou understandest whatever thy heart doeth, this also thy hand doeth, but in a different manner?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Here, however, he added, “These also the Son doeth in like manner.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If He both doeth these, and in like manner doeth, then awake; let the Jew be crushed, let the Christian believe, let the heretic be convinced: The Son is equal to the Father.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “For the Father loveth the Son, and showeth Him all things that Himself doeth.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Here is that “showeth.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Showeth,” as it were, to whom?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Of course, as to one that sees.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We return to that which we cannot explain, how the Word seeth.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Behold, man was made by the Word; but man has eyes, ears, hands, divers members in the body: he is able by the eyes to see, by the ears to hear, by the hands to work; the members are diverse, their offices diverse.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: One member cannot do the office of another; yet, by reason of the unity of the body, the eye sees both for itself and for the ear, and the ear hears for itself and for the eye.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Are we to suppose that something like this holds good in the Word, seeing all things are by Him; and Scripture has said in the psalm, “Understand, ye brutish among the people; and ye fools, at length be wise.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He that planted the ear, shall He not hear?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And He that formed the eye, shall He not see?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hence, if the Word is He that formed the eye, for all things are by the Word; if the Word is He that planted the ear, for all things are by the Word: we cannot say the Word doth not hear, the Word doth not see; lest the psalm reprove us, and say, “Fools, at length be wise.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore, if the Word heareth and seeth, if the Son heareth and seeth, are we yet to search for eyes and ears in Him in separate places?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Does He by one part hear, by another see; and cannot His ear do what His eye doth; and cannot His eye do what His ear can?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Or is He not all sight, all hearing?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Perhaps yes; nay, not perhaps, but truly yes; whilst, however, that seeing of His, and that hearing of His, is in a way far other than it is with us.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Both to see and to hear exist together in the Word: seeing and hearing are not diverse things in Him; but hearing is sight, and sight is hearing.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And we, who see in one way, and hear in another way, how know we this?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We return perhaps to ourselves, if we are not the trangressors to whom it is said, “Return, O trangressors, to your heart.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Return to your heart: why go from yourselves, and perish from yourselves?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Why go the ways of solitude?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: You go astray by wandering: return ye.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: To the Lord. ’Tis quickly done: first return to thine own heart; thou hast wandered abroad an exile from thyself; thou knowest not thyself, and yet thou art asking by whom thou wast made!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Return, return to thy heart, lift thyself away from the body: thy body is thy place of abode; thy heart perceives even by thy body.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But thy body is not what thy heart is; leave even thy body, return to thy heart.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In thy body thou didst find eyes in one place, ears in another place: dost thou find this in thy heart?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Or hast thou not ears in thy heart?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Else of what did the Lord say, “Whoso hath ears to hear, let him hear?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Or hast thou not eyes in thy heart?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Else of what saith the apostle, “The eyes of your heart being enlightened?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Return to thy heart; see there what, it may be, thou canst perceive of God, for in it is the image of God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In the inner man dwelleth Christ, in the inner man art thou renewed after the image of God, in His own image recognize its Author.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: See how all the senses of the body bring intelligence to the heart within of what they have perceived abroad; see how many ministers the one commander within has and what it can do by itself even without these ministers.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The eyes report to the heart things black and white; the ears report to the same heart pleasant and harsh sounds; to the same heart the nostrils announce sweet odors and stenches; to the same heart the taste announces things bitter and sweet; to the same heart the touch announces things smooth and rough; and the heart declares to itself things just and unjust.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thy heart sees and hears and judges all other things perceived by the senses; and, what the senses do not aspire to, discerns things just and unjust, things evil and good.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Show me the eyes, ears, nostrils, of thy heart.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Diverse are the things that are referred to thy heart, yet are there not diverse members there.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In thy flesh, thou hearest in one place, seest in another; in thy heart, where thou seest, there thou hearest.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If this be the image, how much more mightily He whose the image is!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore the Son both heareth and seeth; the Son is both the hearing itself and the seeing: to hear is to Him the same thing as “to be;” and to see is to Him the same thing as “to be.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: To see is not the same thing to thee as to be; for if thou lose thy sight, thou canst be; and if thou lose thy hearing, thou canst be.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Do we think we have knocked?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Is there raised up within us something whereby we may even slightly conjecture whence light may come to us?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It is my opinion, brethren, that when we speak of these things, and meditate upon them, we are exercising ourselves.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And when we are exercising ourselves, and are as it were bent back again by our own weight to our customary thoughts, we are like weak-eyed persons, when they are brought forth to see the light, if perchance they had no sight at all before, and begin in some sort to recover their sight by the assiduous care of physicians.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And when the physician would test the progress of recovery, he tries to show them something which they sought to see, but could not while they were blind: and while the eyesight is now somewhat recovered, they are brought forth to the light; and as they see it, are beaten back in a manner by the very glare; and they answer the physician, as he points out the object, This moment I did see, but now I cannot.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What then does the physician?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He brings them back to their usual ways, and applies the eye-salve to nourish the longing for seeing that which was seen only for a moment, so that by the very longing he may cure more completely; and if any stinging salves are applied for the recovery of sound ness, let the patient bear it bravely, and, inflamed with love of the light, say to himself, When will it be that with strong eyes I shall see what with sore and weak eyes I could not?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He urges the physician, and begs him to heal him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore, brethren, if, it may be, something like this has taken place in your hearts, if somehow you have raised your heart to see the Word, and, beaten back by its light, you have fallen back to your wonted ways; pray the Physician to apply sharp salves, the precepts of righteousness.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: There is that which thou mayest see, but not that whereby thou canst see.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thou didst not believe me before that there is that which thou mayest see: thou art now, as by the guidance of reason, brought to it: thou hast drawn near, strained thine eyes to see it, throbbed, and shrunk back.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thou knowest for certain that there is what thou mayest see, but that thou art not yet meet to see it.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore be healed.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What are the eye-salves?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Do not lie, do not swear falsely, do not commit adultery, do not steal, do not defraud.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But thou art used to these, and it is with some pain thou art drawn away from old habits: this is what bites, but yet heals.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For I tell thee freely, by fear of myself and of thee, if thou give up the healing, and scorn to become meet to enjoy this light, by weakness of thine eyes, thou wilt love darkness; and by loving darkness, wilt remain in darkness; and by remaining in darkness, wilt be cast even into outer darkness: there shall be weeping and gnashing of teeth.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If the love of light has effected nothing in thee, let the fear of pain effect something.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I think I have spoken long enough, and yet I have not concluded the Gospel lesson: if I go on to declare what remains, I shall burden you, and I fear lest even what has been drawn may be lost; therefore let this be enough for you now, beloved.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We are debtors, not now, but always as long as we live; because we live for you.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: However, do you, by good living, comfort this life of ours, so weak, toilsome, and full of peril in this world; do not afflict and wear us out by your evil manners.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For if, when offended with your evil life, we flee from you and separate ourselves from you, and no longer come to you, will ye not complain, and say, And if we were sick, ye might care for us; and if we were weak, ye might have visited us?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Behold, we do care for you; behold, we do visit you; but let it not be with us as you have heard from the apostle, “I fear lest I have bestowed labor upon you in vain.”
